sizing_rule.note: Use options strategies with predetermined maximum loss limits to cap losses at tail-risk events (>3σ). Maximum loss is predetermined with certainty via options structure, not a percentage. Implementation example: 50,000/(5,000/0.1)=1 suggests position sizing derived from max loss constraint.

source_rule_text: Use options strategies with predetermined maximum loss limits to eliminate catastrophic losses exceeding 3 standard deviations, which have higher probability of occurrence than Normal Distribution implies.
